0        1                  ADI                                       NaN       ADI-AI-0035                                               Revamp PrimeFaces to SpringBoot&Angular                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             I have a use case to revamp some PrimeFaces application by migrating it to a Spring Boot backend with an Angular frontend, along with upgrading the platform from Java 8 to Java 21.\n\nThe objective is to modernize the architecture, improve performance, security, and maintainability, and align the application with current standards and long-term support technologies.\n\nPlease let me know if we can proceed with the technical assessment and next steps.                 1/27/2026                                         Development                       Backend-Frontend                                                Code Revamp                                                                                                                                                                             NaN                                                                                                                                                       NaN                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         1. Complex task that takes months to finish which need support\n2. Faster delivery\n3. Open opportunities to gain new projects from customers (Border Guard - GDP - SFES - SFPS) to migrate old JSF projects to SpringBoot                       Not often happens but takes long time and a lot of efforts                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Assessment\n•\tInventory applications and modules\n•\tAnalyze business logic, UI complexity, integrations\n•\tCheck Java 8 → 21 compatibility\n•\tEstimate effort and define roadmap\n\n2. Architecture\n•\tBackend: Spring Boot (REST, layered architecture)\n•\tFrontend: Angular (components, reactive forms)\n•\tDefine API contracts (OpenAPI)\n•\tSet up security (Spring Security)\n\n3. Setup\n•\tConfigure Java 21, Angular environment\n•\tInitialize Spring Boot & Angular projects\n•\tSet up CI/CD (optional)\n\n4. Migration (Incremental)\n•\tKeep existing JSF running\n•\tMigrate modules one by one (start small)\n•\tBuild new APIs + Angular UI\n•\tGradually switch users\n\n5. Refactoring\n•\tMove logic from JSF beans → Spring services\n•\tReplace legacy patterns (EJB, JSF scopes)\n•\tImplement validation & error handling\n\n6. Frontend\n•\tRebuild UI in Angular (no direct migration)\n•\tImplement forms, validation, API integration\n\n7. Java Upgrade\n•\tUpgrade to Java 21\n•\tFix deprecated APIs & update dependencies\n\n8. Testing & Deployment\n•\tUnit & integration testing\n•\tRoll out per module and monitor \n analysts\n• Dynamic Tuning: Model retrains weekly to adapt to evolving threat landscape\n• Analyst Feedback Loop: Security team's manual decisions feed back into model refinement claude     Mohannad Azzi  More Elaboration Needed  Need More Elaboration – Reframe as AI-Assisted API Adapter Generation and Integration Support                                                                Generating service layers, DTOs, mocks, retry logic, and error handling from Swagger/OpenAPI is technically useful, but much of it is still standard code generation that can be addressed through SDK generators, templates, or shared integration libraries. No blocking gaps.                                                                              High feasibility. Code scanning for compliance patterns is a reliable AI use case. Mitigation: compliance team validates all AI-flagged items before blocking a release; maintain false-positive log to tune rules.  1) Define compliance rule database: GDPR data handling, PCI-DSS cardholder data, SOX financial control patterns. 2) Integrate AI compliance scanner into CI/CD as a quality gate. 3) AI flags potential violations with severity and regulatory reference. 4) Developer addresses or documents exception. 5) Compliance team reviews flagged items before release approval. 6) KPI: compliance review time per release; regulatory findings in production.                                                                                  70-80%                                               NaN                                                                                       end of May                                                                           NaN            Accepted Idea                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Accepted – Ready for Pilot   Done                     NaN

          Well-scoped database migration use case.                                     This row is too broad and loosely framed for governed enterprise adoption. “Generate images and videos” is a generic content-production activity, not yet a bounded business use case with clear quality controls, IP policy, reuse model, approval workflow, or measurable enterprise value. 71      72                  EAS                                       ERP        ERP-AI-004                                 AI-Augmented Oracle HCM Cloud Implementation Strategy  This proposal introduces a Knowledge-Based Agent System that serves as an institutional expert across all Oracle HCM implementation phases. The system comprises two foundational components:\n•\tCentralized Knowledge Repository: A structured database containing historical project documentation (BRDs, fit-gap analyses, meeting minutes, MoSCoW prioritizations, future-state process maps, solution designs, technical specifications) and verified government policies from official sources (labor regulations, social insurance requirements, taxation rules, localization standards). Documents are tagged with metadata including industry vertical, public/private sector classification, implemented modules, and country-specific requirements.\n•\tAI Agent Layer: A generative AI system trained on the knowledge repository, capable of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), natural language interaction, document drafting, policy analysis, and predictive risk assessment. The agent operates through defined workflows aligned with implementation methodology phases.\n                     46024                               Entire Project Phases               Productivity Enhancement                                                        NaN                                                                                                                                                                              12                                                                                                                                                         5                        •\tTime saved (estimated): 40% faster project delivery.\n•\tEffort reduced: 30-50% reduction in documentation workload.\n•\tQuality improvement: 40% fewer defects, 60% improved policy compliance, 35% better requirement completeness.\n•\tReduction in errors or rework.\n                                                                              NaN                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             The system leverages vector database for semantic similarity matching. Documents undergo preprocessing: text extraction, embedding generation using OpenAI or Oracle Cohere models, and metadata tagging.\nThe AI agent utilizes a large language model (Oracle Generative AI, Azure OpenAI GPT-4, or AWS) configured for retrieval-augmented generation. When queried, the system retrieves top relevant document chunks, injects them as context into the prompt, and generates responses grounded in organizational knowledge.\n                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Faster kickoff, reduced policy research effort, improved proposal quality.\n\nFaster fit-gap completion, better solution consistency, improved requirement coverage.\n\nFaster configuration setup, fewer errors, quicker data mapping.\n\nFaster test preparation, broader coverage, quicker defect resolution, fewer post-go-live issues.\n\nFaster training preparation, higher adoption, reduced support tickets.\n\nFaster issue resolution, more optimization opportunities, increased managed services value.
